title: TDD Rust core engine, postprocessors, mock provider, and pi provider

do_not_read: other directories in ~/dev unless explicitly referenced

scope: Implement reusable Rust core engine behavior before the server: provider trait, postprocessing, mock provider, pi subprocess provider, prompt rendering, timeout/cancellation, JSON parsing, and repair retry using fake pi tests.
goals: Use the tdd skill: one behavior test at a time, then minimal implementation.
Implement provider abstraction that accepts protocol requests and returns protocol responses or errors.
Implement deterministic mock provider for VS Code/Obsidian UI tests.
Implement postprocessors: strip markdown fences, remove repeated prefix, enforce max chars, reject explanations, normalize newlines.
Implement pi provider subprocess invocation with --no-tools, --no-session, --no-context-files, --mode json, --thinking minimal.
Implement deadline timeout and cancellation handling.
Implement structured JSON parse and one repair retry for malformed provider output.
Redact secrets from provider stdout/stderr diagnostics.
non_goals: Do not implement HTTP endpoints in this task.
Do not implement editor clients in this task.
Do not run real pi smoke tests; use fake pi executable tests.
safety: No autocomplete provider test may permit arbitrary file reads or tools.
Use Cargo for dependencies; no system package installs.
success_criteria: cargo test passes for core/provider/postprocessor slices.
Tests prove pi command arguments disable tools/session/context files.
Tests cover malformed JSON repair retry, overlong output, repeated prefix, explanation rejection, timeout, and cancellation.
